log_error_patterns: Boot image or kernel file not found
No bootable disk — check ISO/disk image path
Could not open a file — check paths in config
Port conflict — VNC/SPICE port already in use
KVM permission denied — run: sudo usermod -aG kvm $USER
KVM not available — check BIOS VT-x/AMD-V setting
KVM init failed — may conflict with another hypervisor
Invalid machine type for this QEMU binary
Accelerator not supported — KVM on wrong arch
KVM not supported for this guest architecture
Not enough RAM — reduce memory_mb or free host RAM
Hugepages not allocated — run: sudo sysctl vm.nr_hugepages=2048
A required file is missing — check disk/ISO/firmware paths
Invalid QEMU argument — check machine type and CPU flags
CPU model not supported by this QEMU version
OVMF/UEFI firmware file not found or wrong format
Virtio device error — may be incompatible with machine type
SDL display init failed — check DISPLAY env var
GTK display init failed — check DISPLAY env var
Socket error — another instance may be running
Network config error — check bridge/network settings
TAP network error — bridge may not exist
SD card interface error — check disk image format
Raspberry Pi machine error — needs kernel8.img + dtb file
ARM machine error — check qemu-system-aarch64 is installed
Host is out of memory — reduce VM memory_mb
QEMU aborted — likely an incompatible argument combination

raspberry_pi_4._notes: Pi 4 emulation is slow. For bare-metal Pi use, flash an SD card instead.

raspberry_pi_3b.description: Emulated Raspberry Pi 3B — serial console only, requires Pi OS image + kernel8.img
raspberry_pi_3b._notes: raspi3b has NO display output in QEMU. Connect via serial console. KVM not available on x86. Needs kernel8.img + bcm2710-rpi-3-b.dtb extracted from Pi OS image.
